evals.prompt: The tests for StringStrategyTests are passing. Can you clean up StringStrategy.cs?
evals.expected_output: All what-comments removed (class/field/method-body comments that state the obvious); structural code unchanged; dotnet test src/ still passes.
expectations: All obvious what-comments are removed (comments describing what code does rather than why)
No structural changes beyond comment removal
dotnet test src/ passes after changes
No files outside StringStrategy.cs were modified

evals.prompt: ListStrategy.cs looks a bit rough from the implement phase — simplify it.
evals.expected_output: new List<T>(length) used instead of new List<T>() — pre-allocating capacity avoids internal reallocations in the hot path; dotnet test src/ still passes.
expectations: List<T> is constructed with capacity hint (new List<T>(length)) rather than default constructor
dotnet test src/ passes after changes
No files outside ListStrategy.cs were modified

evals.prompt: I implemented Gen.Chars() and Gen.Strings() separately and now Gen.cs has the printable ASCII range computed twice inline. Can you simplify?
evals.expected_output: The two inline Enumerable.Range(32, 95)... expressions extracted into a single private static readonly char[] field; dotnet test src/ still passes.
expectations: A single private static readonly field replaces the two duplicate inline Enumerable.Range expressions
Both Gen.Chars() and Gen.Strings() reference that shared field rather than computing inline
dotnet test src/ passes after changes
No files outside Gen.cs were modified
